reforms.description: The Canadian government presented its Innovation Strategy in 2002, aiming to encourage innovation and improve skills across the country, while also implementing service enhancements to streamline access to the Scientific Research and Experimental Development tax credit for researchers and firms.
reforms.package_name: Innovation Strategy and Tax Credit Enhancement
reforms.component_name: Innovation Strategy and Tax Credit Streamlining
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The strategy and enhancements are designed to foster innovation and improve access to funding, which can lead to increased productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Both the Innovation Strategy and the tax credit enhancements have been actively in place since their introduction.
reforms.importance_rationale: The Innovation Strategy represents a structural change in the approach to innovation policy, while the tax credit enhancements significantly improve access for firms.
reforms.source_quote: In 2002 the government presented its Innovation Strategy which has two parts: the first set of goals is focused on encouraging innovation and the second part concentrates on improving skills.

reforms.description: The March 2004 federal budget included additional spending to support the commercialization of research, with funding directed towards CIHR, NSERC, SSHRC, universities, and research hospitals.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This funding is aimed at enhancing the commercialization of research outputs, which can lead to innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The funding was allocated as part of the federal budget and has been utilized since.
reforms.importance_rationale: While significant, this funding is an extension of existing support rather than a structural change.
reforms.source_quote: Additional spending through a range of channels... with special emphasis on support for commercialisation of research.

reforms.description: The March 2004 federal budget allocated C$250 million to purchase shares in the Business Development Bank of Canada, aimed at providing new sources of risk capital to pre-seed and seed stage firms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This investment is intended to enhance the availability of capital for startups, which can stimulate innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The funding was part of the federal budget and has been executed as planned.
reforms.importance_rationale: This allocation is significant but does not represent a fundamental change in policy.
reforms.source_quote: C$250 million to purchase shares in Business Development Bank of Canada, so as to provide new sources of risk capital to pre-seed and seed stage firms.

reforms.description: The Canadian government provided an annual increase of C$90 million to the three federal granting councils that fund basic research, along with an additional C$60 million allocated to Genome Canada to strengthen its research capabilities.
reforms.component_name: Federal Granting Councils and Genome Canada Funding
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing funding for basic research and specific initiatives like Genome Canada is expected to enhance knowledge creation and innovation capacity.
reforms.status_evidence: The funding increases were enacted as part of the federal budget and have been utilized since.
reforms.importance_rationale: This funding increase represents a significant commitment to enhancing the research landscape in Canada.
reforms.source_quote: Funding provided in the 2004 federal budget: annual increase of C$90 million to Canada’s three federal granting councils which fund basic research.

reforms.description: The First Ministers agreed on a 10-year action plan on health, which includes new funding to improve access to care and reduce wait times for essential medical services.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This action plan aims to enhance health services, which can lead to improved health outcomes and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The action plan was agreed upon and has been put into effect as intended.
reforms.importance_rationale: The action plan represents a significant commitment to health sector reform and funding.
reforms.source_quote: The First Ministers met in Ottawa and agreed on a 10-year action plan on health with a view to improving access to care and reducing wait times.
